reviews.review_description: Great game. visuals are amazing and immerses you within 5 minutes. 1 thing though the Polaroid pictures are bright red and broken please fix this.
Challenging and fun.

Great game and graphics. It was shorter than I expected with a bitter sweet ending. Overall, really good game. 
Wondering if there will be a sequel.

I very much enjoyed most of the game. Yes it is short, but I enjoyed the graphics and the voice acting. The endings, however, are bitter with much arguing. There is no happy ending here. I get that not all stories have happy endings, but both endings are filled with misery and unfulfilling. After all that Joel goes through and all that Grace says during the game the only choices involve hurting each other? Angry is the only way to go? A third ending with some happiness would have made this game an easy 5.

developer_response.body: Our intention with that ending was not just to create an unhappy conclusion, but an open-ended one. We believe that when spectators engage with an artwork, they bring their own interpretations to the piece. Therefore, if there is a missing piece at the end, we would love for the user to fill that narrative gap. In our opinion, after a significant conflict like a breakup, the only path to emotional healing is to focus on oneself and provide oneself with what was lacking in the past. We believe that Joel truly begins his REAL journey only at the conclusion of the video game. And so do we.

reviews.review_description: As soon as you start the game you are gifted with beautiful artistic graphics. Then the game shows you some quite original mechanics. It has a mobile base!!! I love that! The puzzles are very well done and their difficulty is balanced. In short, a lovely experience.
Very fun fame, with many puzzles and fascinating story. You can explore a new world while you  pilot a truck and tackle a variety of challenging puzzles to progress through the exciting storyline.
Immerse yourself in an innovative VR game with a captivating storyline, offering a unique and unforgettable experience.
Nice story with good mechanics and easy play. A beautiful visual art with immersive places.

This game is really amazing but it is really short and you can finish the game in an hour or 2, the game has a really good story but it’s just too short, I had a great time with it but at the end it’s kind of a downer

🔥Full Walkthrough on which this review is based available by searching’Gamertag VR’ ‘ Vanishing Grace’🔥

Please be aware this may have some spoilers but I will do my best to avoid details.

The game mechanics work great (no smooth turning), graphics and sounds were nice (great voice work) when it needed to be and the world itself I found pretty interesting. Being on a flying mobile home in the future and looking after that vehicle (even though this could of been made into something much more )while solving the story was pretty cool. 

The puzzles are ok, they rely on you not knowing how to work with the puzzle (tools needed, odd choices) before getting to actually solve the puzzle

Unfortunately as the game played out over the 2-2.5 hours playtime my characters story and his decisions driving the story started to go in a moral direction I cannot agree with. As a player I was then forced to go along with it (like reading a book you don’t want too) if you want to finish the game which I did obviously.

Now normally it shouldn’t bother me this much but my characters reasoning was beyond my thought process so I disliked who I was. Being in VR and playing a game as a character you don’t like is a strange experience in VR. Turns out one of the other main characters I also do not understand or agree with and when I got to the end of the game I felt like the story was a waste of my time. 

I’m completely gutted this is the case but felt 3 stars as correct as technically the game is fine if a bit expensive, I believe $10-12 would been much better and more people would just try the game rather than look for opinions.m

I wasn't sure to buy it because of the bad comments, but I was pleasantly surprised. Yes, the puzzles may be a bit simple, but I truly enjoyed the narrative, the ambient, and the beautiful art.IMO it is totally worth 10 bucks

developer_response.body: Thank you! We appreciate your comment.

reviews.review_description:
I absolutely love this game, the story, artstyle, voice actors.
Even the team that Made this game is super nice and kind.
The game itself is masterpiece and everything about it.
I never Felt like this in any other game (in a good way)
You can feel the characters and Just feel you are in the game.
I just really recomend this 100% its so good.
But if you dont like puzzles and a story games you wont like this one but i completely love this.
I just want more people to play this and i want the team to grow bigger and make more INSANELY good games. :)

developer_response.body: This kind of review means the world to us. Seriously. We are delighted that you enjoyed it that much! Have a wonderful day!!

reviews.review_description: I enjoyed the experience but was left a little disappointed. One of the main things that really threw me off was the lack of interactivity with objects in the game. The devs seem to have made it possible to pick up some objects but not others and it was quite jarring. On the same shelf there will be a bunch of items but you can only pick up one of them for example. Be aware that this is 90% story 10% game. It's got great visuals but the gameplay is mostly find the 3 objects to continue the backing vocal.
i have great internet but it took e hours to download 3.1 GB

developer_response.body: Wow, that's weird hahaha shouldn't be related to the game, but thank you for sharing it and playing!

reviews.review_description: I like the gameplay and  world but the game dose feel short to me with too few chapters and narrow Dialogue options making it seem like it’s rather irrelevant and locations to visit id love to see the craft have a steering Mechanic and explore a vast unknown terrain but I don’t think the Oculus can support that quite yet so I’ll wait and hope the company make a good dlc or sequel

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for sharing your thoughts!!

reviews.review_title: Often glitch and breaks but overall good
reviews.review_description: The game itself is awsome but I have to occasionally restart the game and lose some progress but putting that aside it's a good game

The game has a no challenging aspects to it at all. If you like puzzles and immersive gameplay I highly do not recommend. The overall gameplay wasn’t terrible but this is worth $5-10 I wouldn’t have paid $20 for this had I known it was this short and not as challenging. I simply just got through the game because I thought it’d get more difficult but the ending was also disappointing. Overall, disappointed that I wasted my money :/

developer_response.body: Vanishing Grace aims for good narrative, voice acting and arts in general. In Vanishing Grace puzzles are more a way to acces and walk thourght the aesthetics than a end in itself. Thanks for your comment

reviews.review_description: Hmm yeah the game play was okay, but story hmm it’s over to soon, doesn’t really get resolved, and it feels like the doesn’t really gets started.

Haven’t been able to fully experience the game. Glitches a lot. I’m stuck in the taking a phot at the gas station. Impossible to do it, and the camera keeps taking photos on its own. Patch it up before you sell it boys!

Update: thanks for the tip, but I figured what I had to do (not going to say it here). I rebooted the system, went back and did it again. I keep crashing. Besides that, items keep getting stuck and matrices mixing together. The game can be fun, if we can fix the glitches. 

2/21/21
Downloaded the update. Hands are everywhere and I can’t get off chapter 3. Every time I enter the sleeping quarters all walls disappear and the game has to be restarted. Still a bummer. I was looking forward to experience how the story developed. At this point I’m going online to read it.

developer_response.body: Hey there! Why don't you join our Discord channel so we can talk directly to you to help?? https://discord.gg/S4hXM7Y

reviews.review_title: This game is a beautiful VR must have
reviews.review_description:
Vanishing Grace is a beautiful first game from these developers. The art is beautiful and evocative. The story evolves at a good pace with thoughtful puzzles. There is definite room for growth. I can see the team behind this going deeper into their next game and fleshing out some of the threads that Vanishing Grace doesn’t have time to explore. 

You have to play this game. You have to live it and feel it. It’s what VR was made for. 

Spoilers follow, proceed with caution:




I wasn’t over the moon with the ending. It felt a little hollow and it didn’t come to a satisfying conclusion. So.... here’s my conclusion, see if you agree....

Grace. Isn’t real. The hovercraft was built by Joel. The journey in the game is him retracing the steps of a previous journey. He has created Grace in his mind. That’s why Elena is struggling so much. She knows she can’t stop Joel from going out to the desert again and she doesn’t know how to help with his mental state. Joel was the engineer all along. He’s the one chasing ghosts in the desert. He built whats in the church. Something happened when he returned to the Citadel. He created Grace to explain whatever trauma he’s gone through. Closing to leave Grace in the church, end that relationship, and return to the Citadel is the first step for Joel regaining his grasp on reality. 

The pictures in the hovercraft are not of Grace. They are of Joel and Elena. The photos inside the wedding album would prove this. 

The tapes were actually all blank...

The communications device...... didn’t even have batteries in it.....

developer_response.body:
Cerberus it is a honor for us to feel how you deeply get through the hole experience. You show how things after a second look take a new sense.

Thank you.
